headlines.title: PG&E shuts off power to hundreds in Contra Costa County due to fire risk
headlines.description: With the high winds, low humidity and elevated fire risk, PG&E had turn off power to some parts of the Bay Area with “Public Safety Power Shutoffs” on Sunday. A couple of hundred people in Contra Costa County were impacted, according to fire officials. One of those impacted areas is Camino Tassajara just outside Danville. Power went out on a stretch of the road at around 1 p.m., after PG&E said it was going to cut power to help prevent wildfires. Ranchers in the area were making plans for how to care for their horses over the next couple of hours; some were upset about the loss of power, while others were glad to avoid the fire risk. PG&E says it hopes to restore power to this area by 2 p.m. Monday. PG&E May 16 wildfires May 7 California pushes wildfire preparedness with blazes on the rise

headlines.title: Body recovered after incident at Hellyer County Park in San Jose
headlines.description: A body was recovered following an incident at Cottonwood Lake at Hellyer County Park in San Jose on Sunday afternoon, according to the Santa Clara County Sheriff’s Office. The sheriff’s office said Santa Clara County Park rangers were alerted to a person in distress in the water at around 1:40 p.m. Sheriff’s officials said the San Jose Fire Department also responded to the scene. The sheriff’s office Dive Team and Underwater Search Unit were then called to search the lake, according to authorities. Officials reported a body was found during the search, and the person was pronounced dead at 4:11 p.m. The sheriff’s office said the person’s identity has not been confirmed, and the Office of the Medical Examiner-Coroner will determine the cause and manner of death.

headlines.title: Oakland city administrator resigns due to unprofessional behavior allegations
headlines.description: Oakland’s city administrator resigned after allegations of unprofessional behavior, according to city officials on Sunday. Mayor Barbara Lee says she met with now former City Administrator Jestin Johnson this weekend and accepted his resignation. Lee says she heard about “degrading and unprofessional communications” involving Johnson, so the city conducted an investigation. In a statement, the mayor says that investigation verified those communications that she called “wholly incompatible with the values of this administration and those of the people of Oakland.” She continued to say, “under my watch, I will not tolerate transgressions of this nature.” The assistant city administrator is now serving as acting administrator. NBC Bay Area reached out to Johnson for comment but have not heard back.

headlines.title: Person killed in early morning shooting in San Francisco
headlines.description: A person was shot and killed in San Francisco’s Sunset District early Sunday morning, according to the San Francisco Police Department. Police responded to the 2500 block of 46th Avenue at about 5:20 a.m. after being alerted to the shooting. One victim was found at the scene suffering from gunshot wounds, police said. First aid was given by police officers before paramedics arrived and took over. Despite their efforts, the victim was declared dead at the scene. The death marks the 17th homicide in San Francisco in 2026, roughly double the nine homicides that were recorded in 2025 from Jan. 1 through May 17, according to the San Francisco Police Department’s crime data dashboard.

headlines.title: Vegetation fire off Altamont Pass Road near Livermore burns nearly 70 acres
headlines.description: Firefighters were battling a vegetation fire that burned nearly 70 acres off Altamont Pass Road near Livermore on Sunday afternoon, according to Cal Fire. Just before 12:30 p.m., Cal Fire SCU posted on X firefighters were on the scene of a vegetation fire off Altamont Pass Road, northeast of Livermore. The fire was initially reported to be 3 to 5 acres in size. Half an hour later, Cal Fire said the vegetation fire, which was dubbed the Pass Fire, had grown to reach approximately 80 acres in size. At 1:30 p.m., Cal Fire announced on X forward progress was stopped on the fire and it was 30% contained. A few hours later, Cal Fire said more accurate GPS mapping placed the final acreage of the fire at 69 acres. Authorities added firefighters will remain on the scene working on mop-op and strengthen containment lines.

headlines.title: 3 people killed, several others injured after driver crashes into crowd in Oakland, California
headlines.description: Three people were killed and several others were injured after a driver crashed into multiple cars and pedestrians late Saturday night in Oakland, California, authorities said. The crash happened shortly after 11 p.m., according to officials. Three people were pronounced dead at the scene and five others were injured, the Oakland Fire Department said. Two of those injured were in critical condition. The driver involved in the crash was also injured, though officials described those injuries as minor. Authorities did not immediately release additional details about what led to the crash, and the driver’s identity was not made public. The crash remains under investigation, officials said.

headlines.title: Firefighters rescue recycling facility worker in San Francisco
headlines.description: On Sunday, firefighters rescued a recycling facility worker who was driving heavy machinery when they fell into a recycling pit, according to the fire department. Fire officials said they were dispatched to an incident at the Recology Recycling center at around 11:45 a.m. They said one of the front loaders apparently went off the edge into the recycling pit with the operator inside. Upon arrival, the fire department said crews developed a plan to rescue the worker, who was extricated from the pit almost an hour later. The worker was then taken to the hospital with unspecified injuries.

headlines.title: 3.6 magnitude earthquake strikes near Hollister
headlines.description: A preliminary 3.6 magnitude earthquake struck near Hollister on Saturday night, according to the United States Geological Survey. The quake, which hit at 11:28 p.m., was centered 3.4 miles southeast of Hollister, the USGS said. Further information was not immediately available. This is a developing story. Details may change as more information becomes available. Stay tuned for updates. BAY AREA QUAKE CENTRAL Bay Area Quake Tracker, the latest earthquake stories, extensive quake prep checklists, videos and many other disaster preparedness resources all in one place: NBCBAYAREA.COM/QUAKES

headlines.title: Person dies after vehicle crashes with tree in Sonoma County
headlines.description: A person died and another was taken to the hospital following a solo-vehicle crash in Sonoma County, according to the California Highway Patrol. CHP said the crash occurred on Highway 101, north of Todd Road, just before 4:20 p.m. Authorities said two people were inside a vehicle that crashed with a tree. CHP said the driver died at the scene and the passenger was taken to a hospital with major injuries.

headlines.title: BTS kicks off the first of three performances at Stanford
headlines.description: If you heard cheering near Palo Alto Saturday night, you might have caught wind of the massive BTS concert happening at Stanford Stadium, the first of three sold-out shows there by the K-pop group. Around 50,000 fans made their way to campus to see the global superstars, back together for their first tour since they fulfilled mandatory military service in South Korea. This concert marked the band’s first time back in the Bay Area since 2018. “It’s been so long since they’ve been in the Bay Area, and I feel like Stanford is just an amazing place to be, and I’m just so glad to see them here,” said An Le of San Jose, who drove up to Palo Alto to see the show. “This time, there’s probably a lot more excitement, because this is a big comeback from them,” said Lisa Vang, who drove up from Merced to see the show. Palo Alto is the band’s only stop in Northern California on this tour, which is why some fans booked flights to attend or drove for hours. Music & Musicians May 15 Palo Alto May 14 Music & Musicians May 14 “Oh, I’m getting emotional,” said Jessica Garcia of Visalia, who said as a longtime BTS fan, she was excited to bring her younger sister to a show with her for the first time. As with the Coldplay concert that happened at Stanford Stadium last year, traffic slowed to a bumper-to-bumper crawl in the streets around Stanford before and after the concert. “The freeway was fine, but just coming into Palo Alto, there was like cars everywhere, traffic,” Garcia said. Fans looking for close parking wound up paying $60 to park at the high school across the street, a price they said they were happy to pay to see their favorite band. BTS will perform another show at Stanford on Sunday and a third show on Tuesday.

headlines.title: Firefighters battle two North Bay fires as red flag warnings stay in effect
headlines.description: Firefighters battled two vegetation fires on Saturday as red flags warnings are still in effect in parts of the Bay Area. Firefighters quickly contained a fire that broke out on Rio Vista that burned around 25 acres. The scene was mopped up and cleared by the evening. “That was one of the area’s of concern, because you get really strong winds down there, and you have, in the period that we are in right now, we are starting to see things starting to dry out,” Cal Fire PIO Jason Clay said. The official cause of this fire has not been determined by Solano County fire officials, but a Rio Vista firefighter who helped fight the fire told NBC Bay Area exhaust from farming equipment could have sparked the fire. Firefighters also battle the Tombs Fire near Geyserville in Sonoma County, which burned around 10 acres. The Tombs Fire was rekindled from embers inside a landowner’s burn pile from April. “So that just goes to show the landowner was trying to do the right thing and create some defensible space – add some fire resilience around their property – and the winds sparked up that wildfire,” Clay said. Clay says it’s best to get that work done before red flag warnings are in effect, even if your property isn’t officially inside the warning area. “There’s still fire danger, so even Sonoma and Napa counties, that aren’t under the red flag warning, you know that possibility still exists,” he said. Those red flag warnings remain in effect, especially in the coastal range, throughout the San Joquin Valley and West Side Hills through Monday at 8 p.m. PG&E is monitoring large parts of the region for possible public safety power shutoffs.

headlines.title: Thousands prepare for San Francisco's Bay to Breakers amid windy conditions
headlines.description: One of San Francisco’s most iconic events returns this weekend; thousands of people will either run, jog or walk across the city on Sunday for Bay to Breakers. On the eve of Bay to Breakers, participants gathered at the western side of San Francisco to pick up their bibs. For more than a century, the annual race took participants from one end of San Francisco to the other for a total of 7 and a half miles of fun. Sunday’s race will start on The Embarcadero, where some street closures are already in place. However, this year will be a little different: instead of Ocean Beach, the race has a new finish line. “The finish line is now finishing inside Golden Gate Park, so we’re still the Bay to Break from the Bay to Ocean Beach, but we’re going to walk them out about a quarter mile but still finish in Golden Gate Park with the intention that they can play a little longer”. Organizers say it can get a bit windy the ocean, so they expanded inside near the windmill. In the past, some people jumped in the water to mark their achievement or cool off. This time, firefighters encouraged people to stay out of the water. “Some of these 8 to 10 foot waves that are going to be pushing up on the beach, in addition that we have our strong rip currents, and we have those sneaker waves as well. It’s warming up in the city we’re encouraging people to stay out of the water,” said Samuel Menchaca, SFFD PIO.
